diagrams.title: Propositional Logic Structure and Connectives
diagrams.description: A diagram showing the fundamental components of propositional logic including proposition symbols (P, Q, R), logical connectives (AND, OR, NOT, IMPLIES, EQUIVALENT), and how they form compound propositions. The diagram displays a sample knowledge base with facts and rules connected by these operators.
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes the syntax and structure of propositional logic, helping learners understand how simple propositions combine into complex logical statements used in AI knowledge representation

diagrams.title: First-Order Logic Components
diagrams.description: A diagram illustrating the key elements of first-order logic: quantifiers (∀ for all, ∃ for exists), variables (x, y, z), predicates (e.g., Likes(x, y), Human(x)), constants (John, Mary), and function symbols. Shows example sentences with quantified statements and their meanings.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the enhanced expressive power of first-order logic over propositional logic, essential for AI systems that need to represent relationships between objects and reason about collections of entities

diagrams.title: Logical Inference - Modus Ponens Example
diagrams.description: A diagram showing a step-by-step logical inference process using Modus Ponens. Displays premises (If P then Q, and P is true), the inference rule application, and the resulting conclusion (Q is true). Includes a visual representation of a simple AI knowledge base and query resolution.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates how AI systems perform logical reasoning and inference, showing the mechanical process of deriving new facts from existing knowledge - a fundamental concept for automated reasoning in AI
